notes: Lesson 25 Orientation (act 146) 'System Design — the algorithmic toolbox and the recognition habit', 829.9s, 8 long cues. Uses sysdesign.py (hash_ring/ring_marker/ring_arc/token_bucket/timeline/tick) + inline modulo table / quorum overlap / consensus majority / LRU map+list. Each long ~104s cue uses timed reveals so beats land across the window (verified via muxed start/mid/end frames: cards/table/ticks/nodes build progressively, not bunched). Covers all six tools the parts + later lessons go deep on. 2 -ql review iterations (round-1 caught orientation mutual-overlap defects: Cue05 R+W ineq vs N-label collision, Cue06 majority/minority chips overlapping center — both repositioned); READ start/mid/end each cue, all pass. Math verified: modulo 5/6 reshuffle, ring ≈1/N, R+W>N=3+3>5, 3-of-5 majority.

frames.notes: [start] recognition toolbox: spine 'hear the signal, reach for the tool, defend the trade-off' + 6 signal→tool cards revealed on a timed schedule (Consistent hashing/keys reshuffle, Token bucket/cap rate, Sliding window/no bursts, Quorum/read sees latest write, Consensus/one leader, LRU/O(1) get+evict); closing 'name the tool from the signal'. Cards reveal progressively across the 104s window.

[mid] recognition toolbox: spine 'hear the signal, reach for the tool, defend the trade-off' + 6 signal→tool cards revealed on a timed schedule (Consistent hashing/keys reshuffle, Token bucket/cap rate, Sliding window/no bursts, Quorum/read sees latest write, Consensus/one leader, LRU/O(1) get+evict); closing 'name the tool from the signal'. Cards reveal progressively across the 104s window.

[end] recognition toolbox: spine 'hear the signal, reach for the tool, defend the trade-off' + 6 signal→tool cards revealed on a timed schedule (Consistent hashing/keys reshuffle, Token bucket/cap rate, Sliding window/no bursts, Quorum/read sees latest write, Consensus/one leader, LRU/O(1) get+evict); closing 'name the tool from the signal'. Cards reveal progressively across the 104s window.
[start] server=hash(key) mod N table: keys k1..k6, N=4 (1,2,3,0,3,0) then N=5 (2,2,4,4,1,3) revealed cell-by-cell; 5 of 6 keys jump servers, 'cache-miss storm' note. Modulo math correct.
[mid] server=hash(key) mod N table: keys k1..k6, N=4 (1,2,3,0,3,0) then N=5 (2,2,4,4,1,3) revealed cell-by-cell; 5 of 6 keys jump servers, 'cache-miss storm' note. Modulo math correct.
[end] server=hash(key) mod N table: keys k1..k6, N=4 (1,2,3,0,3,0) then N=5 (2,2,4,4,1,3) revealed cell-by-cell; 5 of 6 keys jump servers, 'cache-miss storm' note. Modulo math correct.

[start] hash ring: servers A@40(blue)/B@160(violet)/C@280(amber) + keys k1@90/k2@210/k3@330 revealed, ownership arcs first-clockwise (k1->B, k2->C, k3->A), then new server D@230(emerald) steals arc 160-230 (k2 recolored emerald), 'add 1 of N -> ≈1/N keys move'. Ownership + steal correct.

[mid] hash ring: servers A@40(blue)/B@160(violet)/C@280(amber) + keys k1@90/k2@210/k3@330 revealed, ownership arcs first-clockwise (k1->B, k2->C, k3->A), then new server D@230(emerald) steals arc 160-230 (k2 recolored emerald), 'add 1 of N -> ≈1/N keys move'. Ownership + steal correct.

[end] hash ring: servers A@40(blue)/B@160(violet)/C@280(amber) + keys k1@90/k2@210/k3@330 revealed, ownership arcs first-clockwise (k1->B, k2->C, k3->A), then new server D@230(emerald) steals arc 160-230 (k2 recolored emerald), 'add 1 of N -> ≈1/N keys move'. Ownership + steal correct.

[start] token bucket capacity 5: state=(count,timestamp); burst of 5 allowed drains to empty; empty->throttled to drip rate; lazy refill tokens<-min(cap,tokens+Δt×rate) refills 3; 'bursts up to capacity friendly, long-run rate is drip rate'. Bucket mechanics correct.

[mid] token bucket capacity 5: state=(count,timestamp); burst of 5 allowed drains to empty; empty->throttled to drip rate; lazy refill tokens<-min(cap,tokens+Δt×rate) refills 3; 'bursts up to capacity friendly, long-run rate is drip rate'. Bucket mechanics correct.

[end] token bucket capacity 5: state=(count,timestamp); burst of 5 allowed drains to empty; empty->throttled to drip rate; lazy refill tokens<-min(cap,tokens+Δt×rate) refills 3; 'bursts up to capacity friendly, long-run rate is drip rate'. Bucket mechanics correct.

[start] sliding-window counter: time axis, 6 ticks revealed one at a time, blue window 'last 60s' encloses exactly 3; 'count = 3 ≤ limit 4 -> allow'; 'no bursts: a 6-in-1s spike would exceed count and be blocked'. Window count matches enclosed ticks.

[mid] sliding-window counter: time axis, 6 ticks revealed one at a time, blue window 'last 60s' encloses exactly 3; 'count = 3 ≤ limit 4 -> allow'; 'no bursts: a 6-in-1s spike would exceed count and be blocked'. Window count matches enclosed ticks.

[end] sliding-window counter: time axis, 6 ticks revealed one at a time, blue window 'last 60s' encloses exactly 3; 'count = 3 ≤ limit 4 -> allow'; 'no bursts: a 6-in-1s spike would exceed count and be blocked'. Window count matches enclosed ticks.

[start] quorum: 5 replicas r1..r5, N=5; write set W={r1,r2,r3} emerald, read set R={r3,r4,r5} blue rings; r3 in both -> overlap; R+W>N : 3+3>5; 'a read always touches a replica that saw the newest write'. Overlap logic correct.

[mid] quorum: 5 replicas r1..r5, N=5; write set W={r1,r2,r3} emerald, read set R={r3,r4,r5} blue rings; r3 in both -> overlap; R+W>N : 3+3>5; 'a read always touches a replica that saw the newest write'. Overlap logic correct.

[end] quorum: 5 replicas r1..r5, N=5; write set W={r1,r2,r3} emerald, read set R={r3,r4,r5} blue rings; r3 in both -> overlap; R+W>N : 3+3>5; 'a read always touches a replica that saw the newest write'. Overlap logic correct.

[start] consensus: 5 nodes n1..n5 split by a rose network-partition wall into {n1,n2,n3} majority (emerald, can commit) and {n4,n5} minority (rose, stalls); 'two leaders can never both hold a majority so never both commit'. Majority/split-brain correct.

[mid] consensus: 5 nodes n1..n5 split by a rose network-partition wall into {n1,n2,n3} majority (emerald, can commit) and {n4,n5} minority (rose, stalls); 'two leaders can never both hold a majority so never both commit'. Majority/split-brain correct.

[end] consensus: 5 nodes n1..n5 split by a rose network-partition wall into {n1,n2,n3} majority (emerald, can commit) and {n4,n5} minority (rose, stalls); 'two leaders can never both hold a majority so never both commit'. Majority/split-brain correct.

[start] LRU cache: hash map (key->node A/B/C violet) above a doubly linked list (A<->B<->C blue, bidirectional arrows) with MRU-front/LRU-back labels; get(k) move-to-front + put(k) add-front-evict-back ops; O(1) get, put, and evict bound. Map+list O(1) design correct.

[mid] LRU cache: hash map (key->node A/B/C violet) above a doubly linked list (A<->B<->C blue, bidirectional arrows) with MRU-front/LRU-back labels; get(k) move-to-front + put(k) add-front-evict-back ops; O(1) get, put, and evict bound. Map+list O(1) design correct.
